source: grill-me session: browser testing integration into claude-code-shared

tasks.title: Make doc-filename.sh ext-optional
tasks.description: Make ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/doc-filename.sh accept an optional 2nd arg (ext). When ext is provided, output stays YYYYMMDD-HHMM-{slug}.{ext} (current behavior, unchanged). When ext is omitted, output YYYYMMDD-HHMM-{slug} with no trailing dot. Must be fully backward compatible: task-filename.sh (which calls doc-filename.sh with ext=json) and every existing caller must keep working. This change enables naming per-run directories for browser-checks (dirs have no extension). Per project memory, no unit tests for claude-code-shared infra; verify by direct invocation and shellcheck.
acceptance_criteria: doc-filename.sh foo md still outputs YYYYMMDD-HHMM-foo.md
doc-filename.sh foo (no ext) outputs YYYYMMDD-HHMM-foo with no trailing dot
task-filename.sh foo still outputs YYYYMMDD-HHMM-foo.json (wrapper unaffected)
shellcheck passes on the modified script

tasks.title: Create app-launch-detection.md shared resource
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/resources/app-launch-detection.md as the single source of truth for how a calling skill (run-tasks, debug) discovers launch context before spawning the browser-checker agent. Define ordered discovery with safe fallbacks for: (1) start_command — project CLAUDE.md, then package.json scripts (dev, then start), then framework defaults; (2) base_url / port — CLAUDE.md, then config files, then framework default (:3000, :5173, etc.); (3) storageState path for auth reuse — playwright.config storageState setting, then a known auth-state file, else none (run unauthenticated and report it); (4) Playwright module resolution — project node_modules first, then a global install (run check.mjs with NODE_PATH=$(npm root -g)), else skip the check and report skipped (never block a task on a missing tool). Mirror the format and altitude of existing resource docs like branching-strategy.md and task-schema.md.
acceptance_criteria: Doc exists at ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/resources/app-launch-detection.md
Has sections for start_command, base_url/port, storageState, and Playwright module resolution
Each rule lists ordered fallbacks ending in a safe default
Playwright resolution explicitly ends in skip-and-report, not block

tasks.title: Create browser-check-result.md result contract
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/resources/browser-check-result.md defining the JSON contract the browser-checker agent returns to its caller. Three statuses: pass (all assertions passed), fail (>=1 assertion failed), skipped (could not run: Playwright module unavailable, or auth/storageState failed). Fields: status, url, assertions (array of {description, passed, detail?}), console_errors (array), artifacts_dir, screenshot (path or null), skipped_reason (string or null). Document how each status maps to caller behavior: pass -> task may go done; fail -> caller edits source and retries within cap, or blocks; skipped -> do not block, report and move on. Include example pass, fail, and skipped payloads. Consumed by browser-checker, run-tasks, and debug.
acceptance_criteria: Doc exists with the full JSON schema and per-field definitions
Three statuses (pass/fail/skipped) defined with caller-behavior mapping
Example pass, fail, and skipped JSON payloads included
Result is specified as JSON

tasks.title: Document browser_verify field in task-schema.md
tasks.description: Add the optional browser_verify field to the task object in ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/resources/task-schema.md. Shape: { "url_path": "/route", "assertions": ["observable behavior", ...] }. null or absent means no browser check. Set at task-creation time for user-facing feature/fix tasks. Document that: (a) e2e-test-authoring tasks (from to-e2e-tasks) do NOT carry browser_verify because TDD runs the Playwright spec directly as its gate; (b) run-tasks gates a browser_verify task as done only when BOTH the TDD acceptance criteria AND the browser check pass, otherwise the task is blocked and HITL-paused.
acceptance_criteria: task-schema.md documents browser_verify shape (url_path + assertions) and semantics
States null/absent = skip browser check
States e2e-authoring tasks omit browser_verify
States the done-gate rule (TDD + browser check both pass)

tasks.title: Create browser-checker agent + register it
tasks.description: Create ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/agents/browser-checker.md and register it in agents/registry.json. Frontmatter model: sonnet, tools: Bash, Write, Read. Stateless single-run agent (one run per spawn; the caller owns the retry loop and server lifecycle). Inputs from caller: base_url, browser_verify spec (url_path + assertions), storageState path or null, Playwright module location (project node_modules or global via NODE_PATH=$(npm root -g)) or unavailable, and a run slug. Behavior: resolve run dir via doc-filename.sh (ext-optional) at docs/browser-checks/YYYYMMDD-HHMM-<slug>/; write an inline node script check.mjs that imports the resolved Playwright module (inline-node always, never write a spec into the repo); load storageState into the browser context when provided, else run unauthenticated; navigate to base_url + url_path; evaluate each assertion; on failure capture screenshot.png and console.log; return JSON per browser-check-result.md. On success clean the run dir; on failure keep it and return absolute paths. If the Playwright module is unavailable, return status skipped. Per project memory, no unit tests for this infra; end-to-end correctness is verified by the smoke follow-up.
acceptance_criteria: agents/browser-checker.md exists with frontmatter model: sonnet and tools: Bash, Write, Read
Registered in agents/registry.json and validate_registry.py passes
Spec describes inline-node-always, storageState auth, docs/browser-checks naming via doc-filename.sh, and the three-status JSON return per browser-check-result.md
Success cleans the run dir; failure keeps it and surfaces absolute paths

tasks.title: Wire browser check into run-tasks orchestration
tasks.description: Integrate browser verification into run-tasks at the orchestration layer, AFTER tdd returns green (placement B; tdd stays untouched). For a task carrying browser_verify: follow app-launch-detection.md to discover launch context; health-check base_url and start the dev server via start_command with run_in_background ONLY if nothing is already responding, polling the port until ready (with timeout); spawn the browser-checker agent against base_url; on pass mark the task done; on fail edit source and re-spawn, with a hard cap of 3 iterations PLUS early bail when two consecutive runs produce identical failures; run-tasks owns and maintains the iteration log (the agent is stateless and just re-runs the same spec). On cap or no-progress bail, mark the task blocked and HITL-pause, surfacing the failing assertions, the iteration log, and the screenshot paths from docs/browser-checks/. Tear down only a server run-tasks itself started; never kill a pre-existing one. A browser_verify task reaches done only when BOTH TDD criteria and the browser check pass. A skipped result does not block. Fold artifact cleanup into the flow (success cleans, failure keeps).
acceptance_criteria: run-tasks detects browser_verify and runs the browser check after tdd returns green
Server lifecycle: start-once / reuse-if-already-up / teardown-only-what-it-started
Retry loop: hard cap 3 + no-progress early bail, loop owned by run-tasks, agent stateless
done gated on TDD + browser pass; fail -> blocked + HITL pause with assertions/log/screenshots; skipped -> no block

tasks.title: Populate browser_verify in to-tasks at creation
tasks.description: Update the to-tasks SKILL.md to populate the browser_verify field on user-facing feature/fix tasks at creation time, using PRD intent (this is the least-arbitrary moment to decide, since to-tasks already scopes each task with full PRD context). Spec shape { url_path, assertions[] } per task-schema.md. Non-UI tasks (pure backend, config, refactor) omit the field entirely. Assertions must be concrete observable behaviors (navigation target, visible elements, redirect targets), not vague prose. Reference task-schema.md as the source of truth; do not inline the schema in the skill.
acceptance_criteria: to-tasks SKILL.md instructs setting browser_verify for user-facing tasks with url_path + assertions
Non-UI tasks explicitly omit browser_verify
References task-schema.md rather than inlining the schema

tasks.title: Integrate browser-checker + CDP MCP into debug skill
tasks.description: Update the debug SKILL.md for all three integration points. (1) Phase 1: when building the headless-browser feedback loop, spawn the browser-checker agent live (it runs on its own, no task prompt, no deferral), passing launch context per app-launch-detection.md; feed its JSON result into the observation log; re-spawn across hypothesis tests. (2) Phase 3 (instrument): document Chrome DevTools MCP as the main-session interactive inspection tool (network waterfall, memory profile, live console, live DOM) for when an automated repro exists but live browser state must be inspected; CDP MCP stays out of the browser-checker subagent. (3) Phase 4: populate browser_verify on the emitted fix task so a later run-tasks session re-confirms the fix in-browser automatically. (4) Fold docs/browser-checks artifact cleanup into the FU-001 post-mortem checklist. Note: this task edits debug SKILL.md, which T-0009 also touches for the rename cross-reference; sequence them on the single branch.
acceptance_criteria: debug Phase 1 documents live browser-checker spawn with result feeding the observation log
debug Phase 3 documents CDP MCP as main-session-only inspection (not in the subagent)
debug Phase 4 fix task carries a browser_verify spec
debug FU-001 cleanup checklist includes docs/browser-checks artifacts

tasks.title: Rename to-e2e-tests skill to to-e2e-tasks
tasks.description: Rename the skill directory skills/to-e2e-tests to skills/to-e2e-tasks to make clear it is a planner that emits tasks, not a test implementer. Update name and description in its SKILL.md (keep it a planner; emphasize the output is a tasks JSON). Update the entry key in resources/skill-tiers.json. Update all cross-references to /to-e2e-tests across the repo (grep the whole repo; known site: debug Phase 4 handoff block). Run register-skill (or sync-skill-tiers.py) to rewire frontmatter and tier. Confirm no dangling to-e2e-tests references remain anywhere.
acceptance_criteria: skills/to-e2e-tasks/ exists and the old skills/to-e2e-tests/ dir is gone
resources/skill-tiers.json updated; sync/register validation passes
grep for to-e2e-tests across the repo returns no stale references
SKILL.md name/description say to-e2e-tasks and emphasize task-list output

tasks.title: Adopt doc-filename.sh in handoff skill
tasks.description: Update the handoff SKILL.md to name its output document via ~/.dotfiles/claude-code-shared/scripts/doc-filename.sh <slug> md instead of any ad-hoc naming, matching the convention used by to-tasks, to-prd-html, and the task files. Independent of the browser-checker work; bundled here because it surfaced during the same session.
acceptance_criteria: handoff SKILL.md calls doc-filename.sh <slug> md for output naming
Output filename follows YYYYMMDD-HHMM-{slug}.md

follow_ups.title: Install global Playwright for the fallback path
steps: Verify global module resolves: NODE_PATH=$(npm root -g) node -e "require('playwright'); console.log('ok')"
Confirm browser-checker's global fallback invokes check.mjs with NODE_PATH=$(npm root -g) so repos without a local Playwright install still run the check

follow_ups.title: End-to-end smoke test of the full browser-checker path
steps: Pick a sample local app with a simple route and a known start command
Create a task with a browser_verify spec (url_path + 2-3 assertions) in a scratch tasks file
Confirm run-tasks discovers launch context, starts the dev server (or reuses a running one), and spawns browser-checker
Confirm a passing check marks the task done and cleans its docs/browser-checks run dir
Induce a failing assertion and confirm the retry loop runs (cap 3), then blocks + HITL-pauses with the screenshot kept in docs/browser-checks/
Confirm a repo without Playwright returns status skipped and does NOT block the task
